core_mechanism: a finite-rank (low-rank) additive correction that projects an approximate, numerically-constructed object onto a set of exact linear constraints — the correction terms being read off from local Taylor-expansion coefficients in a smooth basis — so the corrected object satisfies the constraints exactly while keeping a rigorously bounded residual
brief_reason: The abstract enforces exact local vanishing conditions that the equations and numerics do not preserve by adding analytic low-rank corrections derived from Taylor expansions represented in a smooth basis, i.e. a finite-rank perturbation that snaps the approximate profile onto prescribed constraints with controlled defect.
